evals.prompt: just had the discovery call with Sarah Chen from Bright Coaching. She's a leadership coach based in Shoreditch, works with mid-level managers at tech companies. Brand personality: confident, warm, direct. Not corporate, not wishy-washy, not preachy. Tone is like a straight-talking friend who happens to be really good at coaching. Colours are #FF6B35 orange and #1A1A2E dark navy. She signs off emails 'Speak soon, Sarah'. Pain points: 1) spends ages writing LinkedIn posts, 2) proposals take too long, 3) follow-up emails after discovery calls are inconsistent. She wants the Business package — so 5 skills, 2 agents, 3 commands. Goals: book 3 new clients this quarter, build LinkedIn presence, launch a group programme. Tech comfort is low — she uses a MacBook but isn't technical at all. No writing samples yet but she sent me her website URL.
evals.expected_output: Complete AI Brain folder with 6 core files (CLAUDE.md, BRAND.md, BUSINESS.md, ME.md, PLAN.md, TASKS.md), 5 skills (linkedin-post-writer, proposal-writer, follow-up-writer, social-media-writer, and one more), 2 agents, 3 commands, Cowork global instructions, quick start guide, service summary, and QA report. All personalised to a leadership coach, using coach/consultant industry defaults.

evals.prompt: onboard new client: Dave's Plumbing & Heating, run by Dave Morris in Stratford E15. He's a sole trader, does boiler installs, bathroom refits, and emergency call-outs across East London. Brand is dead simple — no colours, no fonts, he doesn't have a brand guide. Personality: reliable, no-nonsense, friendly. Not flashy, not corporate, not cheap. Tone is like a mate who's really good at his job. He signs off 'Cheers, Dave'. Pain points: following up quotes (loses half his jobs cos he forgets to chase), getting Google reviews, and his invoices are always late. Starter package — 2 skills only, no agents, no commands. Goals: stop losing jobs to late follow-ups and get more 5-star reviews. He's on a Windows PC, not very techy.
evals.expected_output: Complete AI Brain folder with 6 core files, 2 skills only (quote-follow-up and review-reply-handler), NO agents, NO commands (Starter package limits respected). Tradespeople industry defaults applied. Minimal brand data filled from defaults. QA report flags all assumptions.

evals.prompt: build a brain for Henderson & Associates, they're a small accountancy firm in Canary Wharf. Run by Rachel Henderson, she's the Managing Director. 4 staff. They do annual accounts, self-assessment, corporation tax, VAT, and bookkeeping for SMEs. Brand: professional, precise, approachable. Not stuffy, not jargon-heavy, not intimidating. Colours: #003366 navy, #C4A35A gold, #FFFFFF white. Font: they use Montserrat. Tone is authoritative but friendly — they want clients to feel looked after, not lectured. Always use 'your accounts', 'we'll take care of it'. Never use 'tax dodge', 'loophole', 'guaranteed savings'. Sign-off: 'Kind regards, Rachel'. Pain points: 1) client update emails take too long, 2) engagement letters are repetitive, 3) newsletter content is always last minute, 4) staff spend ages writing internal updates, 5) tax deadline reminders go out late. Pro package — wants the lot. 10 skills, agents, commands. Goals: onboard 15 new clients this year, reduce admin time by 50%, launch a monthly newsletter. High tech comfort — Rachel uses a Mac and is very organised.
evals.expected_output: Complete AI Brain folder with 6 core files, up to 10 skills (email-writer, engagement-letter-writer, newsletter-writer, internal-comms-writer, tax-reminder-writer, report-writer, client-update-writer, document-summariser, meeting-summariser, blog-writer), multiple agents (client-comms, compliance, content), multiple commands. Accountancy vertical defaults applied with seasonal awareness. Full Pro package with no limits.
